consent:
RESEARCH INFORMED CONSENT FORM
STUDY TITLE: Language & Cognition (IRB-FY2023-6812)

INVESTIGATOR(S): Marianna Zhang, Mark Ho, Marjorie Rhodes

FUNDING SPONSOR: New York University

INVITATION TO BE A PART OF A RESEARCH STUDY

You are invited to participate in a research study. This form has information to help you decide whether or not you wish to participate - please review it carefully. Your participation is voluntary. Please ask any questions you have about the study or about this form before deciding to participate.

PURPOSE OF THE STUDY

The purpose of this study is to study how language affects how people generalize across a social group. This information could assist in identifying how people form beliefs about groups of people. 

ELIGIBILITY TO PARTICIPATE

You are eligible to participate in this study if you are over the age of 18 and located in the United States.

You should not participate if you are under the age of 18 or located outside of the United States.

DESCRIPTION OF STUDY PROCEDURES

If you agree to participate, you will be asked to listen to statements and answer some questions via an online survey that should take 5-8 minutes. 

RISKS OR DISCOMFORTS

There are minimal risks associated with your participation in this research. Although unlikely, one possible risk from participating in this study is that there may be a breach of your confidentiality and other people may learn about your participation in the study. Please tell the researchers if you believe you are harmed from your participation in the study.

BENEFITS

It is hoped that this study will contribute to knowledge about how people generalize across a social group. 

You are not expected to directly benefit from participation in the study.

COMPENSATION

You will receive $1.63 for completing the survey.

VOLUNTARY PARTICIPATION

Participating in this study is completely voluntary. You may choose not to take part in the study or to stop participating at any time, for any reason, without penalty or negative consequences.

If you withdraw or are withdrawn from the study early, then we will not keep information about you that is already collected.

PRIVACY & DATA CONFIDENTIALITY

Only researchers and others that will keep the information confidential (e.g., regulatory agencies or oversight groups) may access information that could personally identify you.

All data will be stored digitally on a lab server that is only accessible to authorized current lab members via passwords.

Future Use of Data

Information about you collected for this study may be shared with other researchers, used for other research studies, or placed in a data repository. These studies may be similar to this study or completely different. All information that could identify you will be removed before sharing the data or using it for other research studies. We will not ask you for additional permission before sharing the information.

ACCESS TO YOUR STUDY INFORMATION

We will not give you access to the information that is collected about you in this study.

CONTACT INFORMATION

You are encouraged to ask questions at any time during this study. For information about the study, contact Marianna Zhang at marianna.zhang@nyu.edu or (212) 998-7902, or their faculty sponsor, Marjorie Rhodes at marjorie.rhodes@nyu.edu or (212) 998-3546.

If you have questions about your rights as a research participant or if you believe you have been harmed from the research, please contact the NYU Human Research Protection Program at (212) 998-4808 or ask.humansubjects@nyu.edu.

AGREEMENT TO PARTICIPATE

By checking "I consent" below, you are agreeing to participate in this study. Make sure you understand what the study involves before you agree. If you have questions about the study after you agree to participate, you can contact the research team using the information provided above. You may print a copy of this form.
pledge:
Our research aims to discover new findings about how people think. High-quality research depends on high-quality data from real people who engage meaningfully with the task. 

For these reasons, the use of AI tools is prohibited on this task. There are no right or wrong answers to this task; we are interested in whatever comes to your mind. 

Do you commit to engaging meaningfully in this study without the use of AI tools?
